title: US forces enter Iran by..?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify, however intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: US forces enter Iran by April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify; however, intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Note: Only US military personnel who deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran for operational purposes (e.g., military, humanitarian, etc.) will qualify. Pilots who are shot down, or other cases in which US military personnel do not deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran, will not qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: U.S. special forces conducted a confirmed raid into Iranian territory around April 3-5 to rescue crew members from a downed F-15 fighter jet amid escalating airstrikes in the 2026 Iran war, marking the first verified ground entry by active U.S. military personnel. This operation followed joint U.S.-Israeli strikes starting February 28 that targeted Iranian air defenses, missiles, and leadership, hitting over 13,000 sites in 38 days before a two-week ceasefire took effect on April 7, contingent on reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Both sides claim victory, with Defense Secretary Hegseth hailing a "historic" outcome, but traders weigh ceasefire fragility against potential violations or further escalations like Marine Expeditionary Units positioned nearby. Upcoming negotiations and Hormuz monitoring could influence resolution timelines.

title: US x Iran ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another, or for an official ceasefire agreement to be otherwise confirmed to have been reached by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: The US and Iran reached a fragile two-week conditional ceasefire on April 7, 2026, mediated by Pakistan, under which Washington and Israel pause attacks on Iranian targets while Tehran commits to reopening the Strait of Hormuz, averting President Trump's deadline for escalated military action. This eleventh-hour de-escalation, announced via Trump's Truth Social post, triggered sharp oil price drops and equity rallies reflecting trader relief, but tensions persist with Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon prompting Iranian accusations of violations and partial Strait closure. VP JD Vance clarified no Lebanon inclusion, while talks proposed for Islamabad on April 10 aim to negotiate broader terms amid risks of renewed escalation or proxy flare-ups.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain's slight edge in trader consensus at 15.7% implied probability stems from their commanding 3-0 friendly win over Serbia on March 27, reinforcing Euro 2024 momentum with young stars like Lamine Yamal and Pedri driving a fluid attack, while France lurks close at 13.9% after strong recent outings showcasing Mbappé's pace and defensive depth. England's 11.3% reflects a gritty 1-1 draw versus Uruguay, highlighting squad rotation and Southgate's pragmatism amid injury concerns, as defending champions Argentina (9.0%) and Brazil (8.6%) cling to pedigree despite qualifier inconsistencies. The bunched top probabilities underscore the 48-team field's parity post-UEFA playoffs on March 31—where Sweden, Bosnia-Herzegovina, Türkiye, and Czechia advanced—coupled with the expanded group stage's unpredictability, tough knockout paths from the recent final draw, and 10 weeks until kickoff allowing form shifts, injuries, or tactical tweaks to reshape dynamics.

title: Paris Saint-Germain FC vs. Liverpool FC
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Wednesday, April 8, 2026 between Paris Saint-Germain FC and Liverpool FC.

markets.question: Will Paris Saint-Germain FC win on 2026-04-08?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 8, 2026
If Paris Saint-Germain FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Will Paris Saint-Germain FC vs. Liverpool FC end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 8, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Paris Saint-Germain FC dominated Liverpool FC 2-0 in the UEFA Champions League quarter-final first leg at Parc des Princes, with Désiré Doué scoring in the 11th minute and Khvicha Kvaratskhelia adding a second in the 65th off a João Neves assist, driving trader consensus to a 100% implied probability on a PSG win within 90 minutes plus stoppage time. PSG controlled 74% possession and outshot Liverpool 6-0 on target, while the visitors mustered zero efforts on frame amid two yellow cards and a toothless attack. This emphatic clean sheet positions PSG favorably for the Anfield return leg on the aggregate, though rare VAR reversals or UEFA administrative reviews could theoretically challenge the official full-time result.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: A fragile two-week ceasefire between the United States, Israel, and Iran took effect on April 7, 2026, halting direct strikes after 40 days of intense conflict that began with US-Israeli attacks on Iranian nuclear sites, military leadership, and infrastructure on February 28. Tehran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz for safe oil transit, while mediated negotiations are set to begin in Pakistan as early as Friday. However, the truce faces immediate tests: Israel launched major airstrikes on Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon—killing over 250—explicitly excluding that front, prompting Iranian warnings of retaliation and accusations of violations. Traders weigh de-escalation signals against persistent proxy escalations and potential breakdown before the April 21 expiration.

title: The Masters - Winner
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler tops trader consensus at 13.5% implied probability for the 2026 Masters green jacket, driven by his world No. 1 ball-striking prowess, two prior Augusta National victories, and top-20 finishes in every appearance. The bunched top outcomes—Jon Rahm at 8%, Rory McIlroy and Bryson DeChambeau at 6.5%, Xander Schauffele at 5.9%—highlight a wide-open field after J.J. Spaun's surprise Valero Texas Open win and Robert MacIntyre's T2, with no pre-Masters favorite dominating the leaderboard. Rahm's top-5 streak across LIV events, DeChambeau's power game suited to Augusta's length, McIlroy's elite driving distance despite putting inconsistencies, and Schauffele's major consistency amplify competitive dynamics, where course history and strokes gained approach will prove decisive amid withdrawals like Phil Mickelson and Tiger Woods.

title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices California Governor Gavin Newsom as the frontrunner at 27.1% for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, driven by his commanding lead over Kamala Harris in a March California primary poll and ongoing national profile from book tours in early primary states like New Hampshire. Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez holds second at 8.2%, buoyed by her progressive base and recent pledges opposing all U.S. military aid to Israel amid Democratic shifts on foreign policy. Sen. Jon Ossoff ranks third at 6.6% following his viral February anti-Trump speech ahead of 2026 midterms. In this fragmented field post-2024, key differentiators include executive experience for governors like Newsom, youth and ideological appeal for AOC, and swing-state wins for Ossoff; consolidation could hinge on midterm results, Q1 2027 fundraising reports, and endorsements from party elders.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance leads trader consensus at 18.8% implied probability for the 2028 presidential winner, buoyed by dominant showings in recent GOP straw polls like CPAC (53%) and JL Partners (53%), positioning him as the Republican heir apparent amid term limits barring President Trump's reelection. California Governor Gavin Newsom trails closely at 16.9%, reflecting his status as the Democratic frontrunner in early surveys and public signals of interest post-2026 midterms. Senator Marco Rubio's 9.2% share stems from his high-profile role as Secretary of State and buzz from GOP donors. The tight race persists due to the wide-open field, absence of primaries, and uncertainty over 2026 midterm outcomes in battleground states, which could elevate challengers via path-to-victory shifts or Trump endorsements.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil prices plunged over 15% to around $96 per barrel on April 8, 2026, after President Trump postponed threats of strikes on Iranian civilian sites, easing immediate Middle East supply disruption fears that had propelled prices above $110 earlier in the session. This sharp reversal reflects trader relief amid OPEC+ producers, including Saudi Arabia and Russia, announcing modest production adjustments upward on April 5 to support market stability, while U.S. inventories built by 5.5 million barrels to 461.6 million for the week ended March 27, signaling ample supply. Broader demand concerns from slowing global growth and high Treasury yields weigh on sentiment; traders eye weekly EIA data releases and the IEA Oil Market Report later this month for directional cues on April price trajectory.

title: Republican Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as the narrow frontrunner for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination at 49%, edging Vice President J.D. Vance at 39.4%, with Secretary of State Marco Rubio third at 20.8%, reflecting high-volume trading surges on Kennedy's MAHA health reforms that have boosted his populist appeal among GOP base voters skeptical of establishment figures. Recent CPAC straw polls showed Vance dominating at 53% and Rubio surging to 35% amid his foreign policy leadership in the ongoing Iran conflict, yet markets diverge from polling averages by favoring Kennedy's outsider momentum over Vance's incumbency advantage strained by administration war involvement clashing with his prior non-interventionist stance. Separation could arise from President Trump's endorsement, primary polling shifts, or key executive actions before 2028 caucuses.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 40.5% implied probability to win the 2026 NBA Championship, driven by their league-best 62-16 record atop the Western Conference standings with top seed clinched, fueled by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's league-leading win shares and a healthy young core featuring Chet Holmgren dominating the paint. San Antonio Spurs follow at 15.8% after surging to 60-19, second in the West, with Victor Wembanyama's elite two-way play and rookie Stephon Castle's recent triple-doubles boosting their contender status amid favorable playoff positioning. Boston Celtics sit third at 11.8% despite Jaylen Brown's hot streak in recent wins, while Denver Nuggets' 10.5% reflects Nikola Jokić's double-double averages amid tight seeding battles; Detroit Pistons' Eastern Conference lead at 57-21 underpins their 5.5% share as the top East threat in a wide-open postseason path.

title: FC Barcelona vs. Club Atlético de Madrid
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Wednesday, April 8, 2026 between FC Barcelona and Club Atlético de Madrid.

markets.question: Will FC Barcelona win on 2026-04-08?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 8, 2026
If FC Barcelona wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors Club Atlético de Madrid at 100% implied probability in this UEFA Champions League quarterfinal first leg at Spotify Camp Nou, driven by their confirmed 2-0 victory over FC Barcelona on April 8, 2026. Julián Álvarez opened the scoring from a free kick just before halftime, followed by Alexander Sørloth's strike in the 70th minute, capitalizing on Barcelona's reduction to 10 men after a red card that shifted momentum decisively. This stunning away win—mere days after Barcelona's narrow 2-1 La Liga triumph at Atlético—reflects Atlético's clinical counterattacking under Diego Simeone and superior defensive resilience. With the match result official, no realistic scenarios like appeals or overturns threaten resolution to an Atlético victory barring extraordinary disputes.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 98.3% implied probability for no change in the federal funds rate at the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, reflecting the Federal Reserve's steady 3.5%-3.75% target range maintained since the March 17-18 decision amid resilient economic conditions. This strong positioning stems from March's nonfarm payrolls surging 178,000—far exceeding expectations of 59,000—coupled with the unemployment rate dipping to 4.3%, signaling a robust labor market that diminishes near-term rate-cut urgency. Geopolitical oil spikes and Chair Powell's post-meeting emphasis on a "wait-and-see" stance amid inflation uncertainties further anchor expectations for policy continuity. Realistic challenges include unexpectedly weak April data or escalating inflation prompting a 25 basis-point adjustment, though markets see minimal risk ahead of key releases like CPI.

title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Péter Magyar (68.5%) over incumbent Viktor Orbán (31.5%) as Hungary's next prime minister, driven by opposition Tisza party's sustained polling lead ahead of the April 12 parliamentary election. Recent surveys, including Medián's March poll showing Tisza at 58% versus Fidesz's 35% among decided voters and a Reuters median projecting Tisza's two-thirds majority as of April 8, reflect Magyar's surge as a former Orbán ally turned challenger unifying anti-Fidesz sentiment after 16 years of single-party dominance. Voter fatigue, economic concerns, and Tisza's anti-corruption platform have widened the gap, though Hungary's district-based electoral system favors larger parties and turnout could favor Orbán's base. Government formation post-election will determine the outcome.

title: UEFA Champions League Winner
description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League (soccer).

markets.question: Will Real Madrid win the 2025–26 Champions League?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed team will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal and Bayern Munich share 29.5% implied probabilities as co-favorites following their crucial away wins in UEFA Champions League quarterfinal first legs on April 7: Arsenal edged Sporting CP 1-0 with Kai Havertz's stoppage-time header, while Bayern triumphed 2-1 at Real Madrid through Luis Díaz and Harry Kane, despite Kylian Mbappé's reply. These results position both for home second-leg advantages, boosting trader consensus amid their strong knockout form—Arsenal ousting Bayer Leverkusen and Bayern dominating Atalanta in round of 16 ties. PSG's 21.5% reflects defending champions' momentum hosting Liverpool today, keeping the race tight as Atletico Madrid (9%) visits Barcelona, with aggregate scores and potential upsets hanging in the balance before semifinals.

title: Paris Saint-Germain FC vs. Liverpool FC - More Markets
description: More markets for the UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 8 at 3:00 PM ET.

markets.question: Spread: Paris Saint-Germain FC (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 8 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Paris Saint-Germain FC" if Paris Saint-Germain FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Liverpool FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paris Saint-Germain hold a scheduling edge entering their UEFA Champions League quarter-final against Liverpool, with Ligue 1 approving the postponement of their key Lens fixture to allow full rest between the April 8 first leg at Parc des Princes and April 14 return at Anfield. PSG's recent form remains strong atop Ligue 1, but injuries plague their squad: young midfielder Senny Mayulu sidelined with a right calf issue requiring two weeks' treatment, and winger Bradley Barcola out weeks with ankle ligament damage. Liverpool, leading the Premier League, face a grueling run including Premier League clashes on April 4 and 11 around the ties, testing their depth amid solid recent Champions League momentum from a 4-0 second-leg rout of Galatasaray. Home advantage in Paris favors PSG initially, while Anfield's atmosphere could prove decisive in the aggregate.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: U.S. forces conducted airstrikes on military targets at Iran's Kharg Island oil export hub on April 7, 2026—the latest in a series since mid-March—explicitly sparing critical oil infrastructure that handles 90% of Tehran's crude exports, amid the ongoing US-Iran war over Strait of Hormuz access. Vice President JD Vance stated the action signals no strategic shift, while Iran reports full armed forces control, uninterrupted operations, and reinforced defenses. President Trump continues weighing ground seizure options to compel concessions, but no territorial change has occurred. Traders monitor impending ceasefire deadlines and escalation risks, including potential invasions or retaliatory strikes, for any control alterations.

title: LoL: Ninjas in Pyjamas vs Invictus Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Ninjas in Pyjamas and Invictus Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 8 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas win the match against Invictus Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming" if Invictus Gaming win the match against Ninjas in Pyjamas.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 24.5 in Game 1?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Ninjas in Pyjamas and Invictus Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 8 at 5:00AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 25 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: First Blood in Game 2?
markets.description:

markets.question: Game Handicap: NIP (-1.5) vs Invictus Gaming (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Ninjas in Pyjamas and Invictus Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 8 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas wins 2 or more games than Invictus Gaming in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ninjas in Pyjamas and Invictus Gaming collide in a pivotal LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3, both undefeated at 1-0 series records (2-0 games, +2 GD) after Week 1 sweeps that propelled them into the double round-robin's top tier for direct playoff qualification. Carrying momentum from Split 1 top-8 finishes, NiP holds a narrow 7-5 head-to-head series advantage, including a recent March 1 clash, underscoring their competitive parity. No roster changes, benchings, or substitutes reported in the past week; full lineups gear up under Fearless Draft amid a stable patch meta favoring consistent macro play. Traders eye early execution and mid-lane duels, with game difference tiebreakers looming in the tight standings race.

title: Trump announces end of military operations against Iran by ...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President Trump, the US government, or the military publicly and officially announce that their military operations against Iran, initiated on February 28, 2026, have concluded by the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Qualifying statements must clearly indicate that the operation has ended. Informal announcements, statements from unnamed sources or leaks will not qualify. 

Written public statements from Donald Trump (e.g. posts from his personal Truth Social account), will count. Videos posted on his social media accounts will also qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the US government and/or its official representatives; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump announced a conditional two-week ceasefire with Iran on April 7, suspending US airstrikes and bombing in exchange for Tehran reopening the Strait of Hormuz, while declaring military objectives fully achieved after joint US-Israeli operations decimated Iran's air force, navy, and leadership since late February strikes ignited the 2026 Iran war. This de-escalation pause follows recent hits on military targets like Kharg Island and infrastructure, amid fragile diplomacy including Iran's 10-point proposal and Israeli actions straining the truce. The Pentagon stands ready to resume if violated, with negotiations ongoing ahead of the April 21 deadline; no explicit end to operations has been declared, leaving outcome tied to presidential statements and escalation risks.

title: What price will Bitcoin hit in April?

markets.question: Will Bitcoin dip to $60,000 in April?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final Low price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. title: Colombia Presidential Election
description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a nail-biter for Colombia's May 31 presidential election first round and potential June 21 runoff, with Candidate M holding a slim 49.5% implied probability of overall victory ahead of Paloma Valencia (46.6%) and Iván Cepeda Castro (38.5%). Paloma Valencia's dominant March 8 primary win within Centro Democrático propelled her polling surge to second place in late-March surveys, consolidating right-wing voters amid fragmented opposition, while Cepeda benefits from President Petro's recent popularity rebound and Pacto Histórico's legislative edge. Diverging from polls favoring Cepeda's first-round plurality, markets emphasize runoff dynamics favoring moderates like Candidate M wary of polarization. Key upcoming debates, endorsements, and security developments could separate frontrunners in this closely contested race.

title: LoL: Dplus KIA vs Hanwha Life Esports (BO3) - LCK Rounds 1-2
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Dplus KIA and Hanwha Life Esports in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 8 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Dplus KIA" if Dplus KIA win the match against Hanwha Life Esports.

This market will resolve to "Hanwha Life Esports" if Hanwha Life Esports win the match against Dplus KIA.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 31.5 in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Dplus KIA and Hanwha Life Esports in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 8 at 4:00AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 32 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: First Blood in Game 1?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Dplus KIA and Hanwha Life Esports in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 8 at 4:00AM ET:

This market will resolve to the team that secures the first blood in Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to stoppage. If the first blood did not occur, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to the remake. If the first blood did not occur prior to the remake, resolution will be based on the remade game.

If Game 1 is completed but first blood never occurred, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game Handicap: HLE (-1.5) vs Dplus KIA (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Dplus KIA and Hanwha Life Esports in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 8 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Hanwha Life Esports" if Hanwha Life Esports wins 2 or more games than Dplus KIA in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Dplus KIA". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Dplus KIA and Hanwha Life Esports each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Dplus KIA and Hanwha Life Esports each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both Dplus KIA and Hanwha Life Esports. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hanwha Life Esports holds a slight edge in trader consensus for this LCK Rounds 1-2 BO3 matchup, buoyed by their 2-1 record featuring dominant wins like a 2-0 sweep over HANJIN BRION and resilient performances from stars Zeus, Kanavi, Zeka, Gumayusi, and Delight, whose aggressive early-game style thrives in the current patch. Dplus KIA sits at 1-2 after a clean 2-0 over Kiwoom DRX but losses to frontrunners Nongshim RedForce and others, with ShowMaker anchoring midlane amid newer bot duo Smash and Career still gelling. Even 8-8 head-to-head history underscores a competitive clash, but HLE's jungle pressure from Kanavi and overall momentum from recent series drive the implied probabilities.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices "No" at 97% for the Iranian regime falling by April 30, reflecting the leadership's resilience following the February 28 U.S.-Israeli strikes that killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and sparked the brief 2026 Iran war. A ceasefire via early April Islamabad Accords halted escalation, enabling son Mojtaba Khamenei's transition amid health rumors but confirmed public statements. Harsh crackdowns, including recent executions of protesters and over 1,500 arrests by late March, have suppressed ongoing unrest from January's Rasht massacre and economic distress, with no verified mass defections or IRGC fractures. While leadership instability or renewed protests could shift odds, the regime's repressive apparatus maintains control absent a major catalyst in the next three weeks.

title: LoL: Top Esports vs Team WE (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Top Esports and Team WE in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 8 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Top Esports" if Top Esports win the match against Team WE.

This market will resolve to "Team WE" if Team WE win the match against Top Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and Team WE each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and Team WE each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and Team WE each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 2. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Top Esports enters this LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 with momentum from a 2-0 sweep over Anyone's Legend three days ago, where top laner Creme and jungler Tian excelled in skirmishes and macro execution, positioning TES at 1-1 and tied for third in early standings. Team WE, conversely, stumbled 0-2 to Ninjas in Pyjamas in their opener, exposing weaknesses in teamfights and objective trades, leaving them winless at the bottom. TES dominated WE 2-0 in Split 1 playoffs under similar Fearless Draft rules, bolstering trader consensus on their superior roster depth and patch 26.7 alignment favoring aggressive early-game styles, though WE's mid laner Karis remains a wildcard for laning upsets.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 3 - April 10, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 3 12:00 PM ET to April 10, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket clusters around 240-259 X posts by Elon Musk from April 3 12:00 PM ET to April 10, with 39.5% implied probability edging out 220-239 at 32%, reflecting his verified 179-post tally through day six per XTracker data—a steady 30-per-day pace after a subdued April 3 (just three posts) and subsequent ramp-up fueled by viral memes, Tesla inventory alerts, and xAI model teases like upcoming 1T-parameter Grok variants. The neck-and-neck leading bins underscore competitive dynamics driven by Elon's notorious posting volatility, where political commentary or SpaceX updates could spike volume to 260+ in the final two days, while lulls might cap below 240; watch April 9-10 for engagement surges amid ongoing cultural buzz around his tech empire.

title: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Iran by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Iran or the United States as of November 4, 2025 12:00 PM ET, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

markets.question: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Iran by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: A two-week ceasefire between the US, Israel, and Iran, announced April 7, 2026, by Iran's Supreme National Security Council with US backing, marks the dominant de-escalation signal driving trader consensus to a 65% implied probability against a US ground invasion before 2027. This follows March escalations—including President Trump's threats of intense strikes, Pentagon plans for limited operations on islands like Kharg to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and deployments of 5,000-plus Marines and paratroopers amid ongoing US-Israeli airstrikes—but no full-scale ground commitment has materialized despite Iranian warnings. Negotiations now focus on nuclear constraints and sanctions relief, underscoring diplomatic off-ramps amid historical aversion to major Middle East invasions, though troop postures and rapid reversals keep odds competitive.

title: Thunder vs. Clippers
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 8 at 10:00PM ET:
If the Thunder win, the market will resolve to "Thunder".
If the Clippers win, the market will resolve to "Clippers".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder (63-16), locked into the Western Conference's top seed, visit the playoff-hopeful LA Clippers (41-38) at Intuit Dome on a late-season Tuesday night, with OKC's elite defense (107.3 PPG allowed) anchoring trader consensus amid their dominant form. Jalen Williams returns from right hamstring injury management after sitting Monday, bolstering a near-full Thunder roster (only Thomas Sorber out, torn ACL), while Clippers confirm Kawhi Leonard available on the second night of a back-to-back but rule out Bradley Beal (left hip), Darius Garland (toe), Isaiah Jackson (ankle), and Yanic Konan Niederhäuser (Lisfranc). Thunder won their lone head-to-head this season 122-101 in December; LA's home-court and play-in urgency add upset potential, but OKC's depth and momentum favor them.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pittsburgh Pirates host the San Diego Padres in the series finale at PNC Park, with right-hander Mitch Keller (quality starts in both outings) facing Michael King (3.38 ERA over 10 2/3 innings). Pirates capitalized on a five-run eighth inning for a 7-1 victory yesterday, halting San Diego's three-game win streak after blanking Pittsburgh 5-0 on April 6. Padres hold a dominant 12-1 edge in the last 14 head-to-heads but enter with a depleted rotation amid multiple injuries, including Joe Musgrove's setback and Yu Darvish on the injured list alongside Griffin Canning and others. Early-season NL Central-leading Pirates benefit from home-field and momentum, while Padres rely on lineup strength from Fernando Tatis Jr. and Manny Machado.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona top the La Liga table with 73 points from 29 matches, seven points clear of Real Madrid after a dominant run including a 3-0 Copa del Rey win over Atlético Madrid on March 3—though Los Colchoneros advanced on aggregate to the final. Heading into the April 4 clash at the Metropolitano, trader consensus hinges on Barça's attacking firepower led by Lamine Yamal and Pedri amid Raphinha's fresh right hamstring injury from Brazil duty, potentially joining doubts over full-backs Koundé and Balde. Atlético, fourth in standings, face absences like goalkeeper Jan Oblak (muscle strain) and midfielder Pablo Barrios (thigh), but boast home strength under Simeone and momentum from a recent Madrid derby loss. Recent form favors Barça's away resilience, while Atlético's defensive setup could exploit any visitor fatigue.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen top trader consensus at 38.9% implied probability to win Eurovision 2026 with their UMK-winning entry "Liekinheitin," a striking fusion of classical violin and pop energy that dominated Finland's national final on February 28 and has since captured jury appeal and televote buzz through viral performances and OGAE fan votes. France's Monroe follows at 11% with "Regarde!," surging in late March betting markets after strong precursor polling among Big Four nations, while Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund holds third at 10.1% on the anthemic "Før vi går hjem" from their February DMGP victory. Momentum favors Finland's balanced jury-televote potential in Vienna's May 12-16 contest, though late previews and running order draws could shift dynamics ahead of semi-finals.
